episode_name: Core raid window and firestorm deaths in Dresden proper
geographic_scope: Dresden proper during the 13-15 February 1945 Allied raids
theater_description: The focal episode is the 37-hour sequence of four Allied air raids on Dresden and the immediate direct bombing/firestorm deaths in the city proper.
geometry_method_note: Restricted to Dresden proper and the core raid window, excluding broader campaign mortality and later missing-person politics.
military_area_note: The city included military personnel, POWs, and forced laborers as well as civilians and refugees, but the current source set does not support a clean military denominator or death split.
civilian_area_note: The number and composition of civilians physically present in Dresden during the raid window is unresolved because of refugee inflows and wartime movement.
split_reason: A single raid-window row is preferred because the official commission's strongest findings refer to the full 13-15 February sequence.
quality_note: Official commission evidence is strong on direct-death totals and on rejecting inflated mythic numbers, but weak on denominator and status disaggregation.
